title: Pentacalia nigrostagnosa
taxonomy: Pentacalia nigrostagnona is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Ecuador. The classification of Pentacalia nigrostagnona includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. Pentacalia nigrostagnona is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida and order Asterales. At the family level, it belongs to Asteraceae.

intro: Pentacalia nigrostagnosa, also known as black swamp pentacalia, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its dark green, wavy leaves and small white flowers. The leaves and stems of Pentacalia nigrostagnona are rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Pentacalia nigrostagnona is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its therapeutic properties.
